notes: Insulation hatch fill on X-평면도(기본형)$0$INSUL: 24 uniform 5-vertex LWPOLYLINE cells (from handle 4F0D); wall-adjacent fill, no wall-line geometry.

rationale.evidence: All 24 entities are LWPOLYLINE on layer X-평면도(기본형)$0$INSUL, each with exactly 5 vertices (uniform closed-quad cells), starting at handle 4F0D. The layer's terminal token INSUL marks insulation, and the block carries no LINE, TEXT, DIMENSION, or INSERT entities. A dense set of identical small closed cells is the drawing signature of an insulation hatch fill rather than discrete wall drafting.
rationale.rule: A block whose entities are exclusively small, uniform, closed LWPOLYLINEs on an *$INSUL layer is an insulation hatch fill; in plan view insulation sits inside the wall cavity, so it reliably co-locates with a wall yet is not itself the wall boundary line.
rationale.counterfactual: Had these entities been on a WALL/벽 layer, or had the block also carried long 2-point LINE pairs tracing parallel offset faces, I would raise wall_likelihood and list those as wall_line_handles; a TEXT/DIMENSION or door-swing INSERT would instead push the role toward 치수캐시/심볼.
rationale.uncertainty: This projection derives no vertex coordinates or bbox (all-LWPOLYLINE, no LINE start/end), so cell size, run length, orientation, and whether this is wall vs. slab/roof insulation cannot be confirmed — only layer semantics and entity uniformity are observable.

notes: Flagpole (국기게양대) marker symbol: WIPEOUT mask + named INSERT + thin plaque frame on layer 0 (bbox ~1220x126); not walls.

rationale.evidence: The block contains an INSERT (handle 52A0) of block X-평면도(기본형)$0$국기게양대 — 국기게양대 = national-flag hoisting mast — plus 2 WIPEOUTs (529E/529F) that mask background and 20 LINEs on layer 0 (handles 52A1–52B4) forming a nested thin frame roughly 1220 wide by 126 tall with a central 1176-long bar only ~50 tall. Masking + a named-fixture INSERT + a small closed plaque frame is the signature of a plan symbol/marker, not building fabric.
rationale.rule: A block combining WIPEOUT masking, a named-fixture INSERT, and a small closed frame on layer 0 is an annotation symbol/marker; symbols are self-contained fixtures, not wall runs, so wall_likelihood stays low even when some frame lines are long and thin.
rationale.counterfactual: If the WIPEOUTs and the 국기게양대 INSERT were absent and the LINEs instead formed two long parallel offset faces on a wall layer spanning meters, I would reclassify as 평면 부분도 and mark those lines as wall candidates.
rationale.uncertainty: I cannot see the inserted block's internal geometry or its placement/scale on the sheet, so the real-world size of the marker and whether the thin bar happens to abut a wall are not determinable from this projection.
